blocks.content: The conversation with her parents happened that evening.

She hadn't planned it. She'd come home with every intention of making dinner and going to bed early, and instead found her mother at the kitchen table with two cups of tea already made and the expression of a woman who has been waiting without appearing to wait.
Her father was in the adjacent room with a book open on his lap but not reading.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Sit down.
Not an order. An invitation placed carefully in advance.
She sat.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: You've been carrying more than usual. These last few weeks.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Is it that obvious?

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Only to us. And Lena, probably.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Lena already had tea with me this morning.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Good. Tell me where you are.

Cassandra looked at the table. There was a small chip in the edge of the wood that she'd made when she was nine, dropping a heavy book, and she had always run her thumb along it without meaning to. She ran her thumb along it now.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I'm somewhere I didn't expect to be. With Ethan. It's real — that's not the issue. The issue is that it's real enough now that I have to do the difficult thing soon. And I keep finding reasons to wait.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: What kind of reasons?

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Good ones. The wrong reasons.
She looked up.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I want more time in the version where he looks at me like I'm uncomplicated.
Her mother was quiet for a long moment. Then she reached across the table and placed her hand over Cassandra's. Not gripping. Just placing, like a gentle weight.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: You have never been uncomplicated. Not from the day you were born. You came into this world with an intensity, this need to pay attention to things most people walk past. That was you before any of this. That will be you after.

blocks.speaker: Mother
blocks.content: Don't sell yourself as a simplified version. Not even to protect him from the complicated part.
